rationale: Storage deficit tracking 140 Bcf below 5-year average heading into withdrawal season with persistent cold weather risk in the Northeast. Entered long at market open after third consecutive above-seasonal storage withdrawal confirmed the thesis.
reflection: Trade performed as hypothesized. The storage setup drove the move; managed-money positioning extended rather than liquidating as feared. Exited slightly below take-profit target — no regrets on the early close given the elevated positioning risk.

rationale: Three consecutive above-consensus EIA builds and stretched managed-money net longs at 1.8 standard deviations above 2-year seasonal average. Warm temperature outlook provides the near-term catalyst.
reflection: Mean-reversion trade worked cleanly. The COT signal was the most differentiated factor. Should have held slightly longer toward the 3.05 target rather than taking profits at 3.09.

rationale: LNG export capacity ramp-up thesis: new Gulf Coast train approaching commercial operations, which should add ~0.8 Bcfd of feedgas demand by late June. Targeting summer injection season.
